body.title: One-time content merge of drifted survivor skills to canonical
body.intention: I intend that each surviving workflow skill has exactly one forward-canonical version in the repo, so the single-source flip can't freeze a stale copy.
body.description:
**Problem:** the surviving workflow skills are drifted between local and repo across 4 axes; pick one forward-canonical version of each BEFORE single-sourcing (flip-before-merge would freeze a stale-on-some-axis copy). Also gated behind the prune ticket so only the FINAL survivor set is merged.

**Shape:** For each drifted survivor, merge to forward-canonical: theigors/->unseenuniversity/ (palace paths), /decided->/sorted (command), ~/TheIgors->~/dev/src/UnseenUniversity & ~/.TheIgors->~/.unseen_university (roots). KEEP sorted's decisions_log.dsb append (T-decisions-dsb-cutover still OPEN; context-load still reads the .dsb fallback). Union features where copies diverged. Most survivors are mechanical 4-axis find-replace; only ~3 need feature-merge judgment: day-close (gate-sweep + prune-emissions + discussion note), sprint-ticket (EvaluatorDevice grader), autocompact (the trimmed local version, NOT the bloated repo one). Land canonical in repo skills/. Env-var axis (IGOR_HOME_DB_URL->UU_HOME_DB_URL) is LEFT to the gated T-uu-sweep-* sweeps — do NOT hand-edit env vars here.

**Affected files:** skills/ (the ~19 drifted survivors minus those removed in the retire/prune tickets). Ref: docs/skills_intention_review_20260623.md, docs/skills_harmony_recommendation_20260622.md.
**Design rules:** behavior-preserving per skill; env-var de-hardcoding deferred to the sweeps (no double-work).
**Scope boundary:** IN — content merge of survivors to canonical repo copies. OUT — the flip to single-source (T-skills-single-source-flip); env-var de-hardcoding (T-uu-sweep-*).
**Test plan:** per skill, repo copy reflects forward forms; spot-run that day-close, autocompact, sprint-ticket SKILL.md still parse / load.
**Completion criteria:** `git grep -nE 'theigors/|/decided|~/TheIgors|~/.TheIgors' skills/` returns zero in survivors (excluding sorted's intentional .dsb line); each survivor is a single agreed version; the 3 feature-merge skills retain ALL features from both copies.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
…and 38 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: Resolved to repo-canonical: repo skills/ verified as the clean fs-store rewrite (D-canonical-memory-consolidation); local 'survivor' divergences confirmed deprecated cruft, not novel logic (spot-checked context-load/workflow/day-close-audit; stale-marker scan across all; hygiene guard green = no 4-axis drift in repo). Full local backup retained. Flip (T-skills-single-source-flip) shipped on this basis.
body.unproven_reason: No executable harness asserts 'repo content superset of all live local content' — superset was verified by inspection + retained backup, not a red/green test. Lever: a content-completeness check across the two trees.
